header.search_hint: Type to search, press Enter to go to devices page

step1.title: Authenticate to continue
step1.subtitle: Enter your credentials to access the network operations center.

step2.title: Verify your identity
step2.hint_totp: Enter the <1>6-digit code</1> from your authenticator app.
step2.hint_email_pre: Click the button below to send a verification code to your email.
step2.hint_email_post: Enter the <1>6-digit code</1> sent to your email.

method.email_sub_default: Sent to your registered email address
method.recovery_sub: One-time use · provided during MFA enrollment

step3.title: Access granted
step3.subtitle: Connecting to the network operations center.

err.no_challenge: No verification session — please sign in again
err.expired: Verification expired — please sign in again

dashboard.title: Network Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Network overview and summary
dashboard.qa_add_device_sub: Add a new device to your network

ticker.label: LIVE FEED
ticker.empty: No events in the last 30 minutes

dashboard.empty_grid: All widgets are hidden. Use Customize → Widget Visibility to pick some.

card.probes_caption: Check the dedicated page for probe results

mission.rail_empty: No events in the last 30 minutes

editorial.headline_stable: The fleet is stable today; all devices online.
editorial.headline_few: The fleet is largely stable; a few devices are being watched.
editorial.headline_many: The fleet has offline devices that need attention.
editorial.body_events: <s>{{count}} events</s> recorded in the last 24 hours.
editorial.stat_incident_open: {{count}} OPEN, service affected. Most critical service: {{name}}.
editorial.stat_incident_none: No open incidents; all services stable.
editorial.stat_experience_note: Synthetic probe + uptime + packet loss components.
editorial.stat_anomaly_none: No anomalies; fleet behavior is normal.
editorial.brief_service_none: No affected services — all services stable.
editorial.brief_attention_none: No noteworthy attention point for today.

devices.title: Devices
devices.bulk_fetch_info_confirm: Fetch info for {{count}} devices via SSH?
devices.bulk_delete_desc: Selected devices and backup data will be permanently deleted.
devices.delete_confirm_desc: Backup data will also be deleted.
devices.bulk_agent_info: The proxy agent of selected devices will be changed. Leave empty to use direct SSH.
devices.cred_update_info: SSH password and enable secret of selected devices will be updated.
devices.subtitle: Multi-vendor inventory — SSH connectivity test, automatic backup, vendor detection and agent assignment.

card.archive_desc: Device is moved to archive (restore only by super_admin). Continue?

toolbar.group_profile_tooltip: Bulk-assign credential profile to all devices in a group

form.hostname_tooltip: If left empty, the IP address is used. Can be auto-fetched from the device with the 'Fetch Info' button.
form.alias_tooltip: Personal alias — shown in search and lists
form.layer_tooltip: Used for layer-based filtering in the topology view
form.org_location_tooltip_edit: Use the "Move Location" action in the device list to change location
form.org_location_tooltip_new: The device is created in the selected location. On success, the list filters to that location.
form.readonly_tooltip: When active, only show/ping commands are allowed. When disabled, config commands can run (except denylist).
form.approval_tooltip: When active, medium/high-risk config commands go to admin approval — four-eyes principle.
form.cred_profile_tooltip: If a profile is selected, it is used for SSH/SNMP connections; per-device fields stay secondary.
form.cred_profile_alert: Credential profile active — the SSH/SNMP fields below are only used as fallback when the profile lacks a value.
form.agent_tooltip: If an agent is selected, SSH commands are routed through it instead of going direct.
form.fallback_agents_tooltip: Tried in order when the primary agent is offline. Can be left empty.
form.placeholder_profile_override: (profile value is used; override if needed)

wizard.title: New Device Wizard
wizard.step0_alert: IP address is required. Hostname can be left empty and auto-fetched in the final step.
wizard.hostname_tooltip: You may leave it empty — it can be auto-fetched in the final step.
wizard.alias_tooltip: Shown in the list and search
wizard.layer_tooltip_short: For the layer filter in the topology view
wizard.cred_profile_tooltip: If a profile is selected, it is used for SSH/SNMP connections; the fields below stay as fallback.
wizard.cred_profile_alert: Credential profile active — used for SSH/SNMP connections.
wizard.agent_tooltip: SSH commands are routed through this agent (optional)
wizard.fallback_tooltip: Tried in order when the primary agent is offline
wizard.readonly_tooltip_short: When active, only show/ping commands are allowed.
wizard.approval_tooltip_short: When active, config commands go to admin approval.
wizard.snmp_alert: SNMP can be disabled — it can be enabled later from Device Settings.
wizard.summary_alert: Review the summary below, then create the device and test the SSH connection.

move.title: Move Device — {{hostname}}
move.warning_title: The device's location ownership changes only through this action.
move.warning_desc: The action is written to the audit log; the device's historical data is also moved to the new location.
move.toast_success: Device moved to the new location

group_profile.title: Assign Credential Profile to Group
group_profile.alert_title: The credential profile of all devices in the chosen group is updated in bulk.
group_profile.alert_desc: If a device has an individual profile assignment, this action overrides it.
group_profile.profile_help_empty: If left empty, the profile assignment of devices in the group is removed.
group_profile.toast_assigned: Credential profile assigned to {{count}} devices in {{group}}: "{{profile}}"
group_profile.toast_removed: Credential profile removed from {{count}} devices in {{group}}

auto_group.title: Automatic Grouping Suggestions
auto_group.empty: Not enough common attributes for grouping. Define site, layer or topology links on devices.

bulk_move.title: Bulk Move Location — {{count}} devices
bulk_move.warning_desc: Target location must be in the SAME organization as the source device. Insufficient permission or cross-org devices are automatically skipped.

bulk_tag.title: Bulk Tag Action — {{count}} devices
bulk_tag.input_placeholder: Enter a tag or pick from existing tags
bulk_tag.hint_add: This tag will be added to {{count}} selected devices
bulk_tag.hint_remove: This tag will be removed from {{count}} selected devices

csv.intro_title: Add or update hundreds of devices in one go via CSV.
csv.intro_desc: Matching is done on ip_address — same-IP devices are updated, new IPs are added.
csv.dropzone: Drag CSV file here or click

detail.not_found_desc: Device has been deleted, belongs to another org, or you have no access.

toast.backup_no_change: Config unchanged, no new backup taken

actions.backup_tooltip: Take a config backup from the device over SSH
actions.ssh_tooltip: Switch to Terminal tab (pick Live SSH mode for Live SSH)
actions.refresh_tooltip: Refetch all data for this device

overview.cpu_ram_no_data: CPU/RAM not available — SNMP is enabled but no data may be coming yet.

ports.dry_run_tooltip: DRY-RUN recommendation [policy={{policy}}] · {{count}} flap events (24h), latest: {{trans}} port flips. Real action NOT applied (shutdown coming in C5).
ports.unreachable_desc: Policy overrides can still be saved; effective values apply once the device is reachable. Bulk actions remain open.
ports.readonly_alert: Read-only view. Bulk assignment and override removal require org_admin+ role.

row.vlan_tooltip: Assign VLAN to this port (single-port quick path)
row.poe_off_desc: The device on this port will lose power. The action is permanent.

toolbar.no_override_selected: None of the selected ports have an override
toolbar.override_remove_count: Override will be removed on {{count}} ports (unassigned ones skipped)

bulk_poe.on_title: Turn PoE On on {{count}} selected ports?
bulk_poe.on_desc: The action is permanent. PoE-incompatible ports are skipped.
bulk_poe.off_title: Turn PoE Off on {{count}} selected ports?
bulk_poe.off_desc: Devices on these ports will lose power. The action is permanent.

toast.poe_permanent: The action is permanent. Apply the inverse to undo.

vlan_modal.title: {{iface}} → Assign VLAN
vlan_modal.native_extra: If empty, the vendor default applies (Cisco/Ruijie: 1).

vlan.delete_confirm_desc: VLAN {{vlan_id}} ({{name}}) will be removed from the device.

poe.no_info_title: This device has no PoE info
poe.no_info_desc: Device does not support PoE, no data collected yet, or endpoint did not respond. Data is expected for PoE-capable switches via SNMP poll.

backup.drift_desc: Current device configuration differs from the golden baseline:
backup.readonly_hint: Taking backups and marking golden require org_admin+ role; download + content view + diff are open to all.
backup.preview_empty: Content is empty or could not be loaded.
backup.policy_desc: Offline policy scan on this backup snapshot — no live device access required.

live_config.title: Live running-config
live_config.scan_tooltip_no_config: Device could not fetch live config; if triggered, backend will re-attempt over SSH.
live_config.scan_tooltip_default: Scans the running-config against policy rules.

diff.title: Config Diff
diff.no_changes_title: No changes between the two backups
diff.no_changes_desc: The two configurations may have different hashes but the line-based diff is empty.

security.switch_policy_desc: Device CPU/memory/PoE thresholds etc. — empty = unassigned → org default.
security.port_policy_desc: Default for all device ports. Per-port override = Ports tab.
security.resolver_desc: Where the applied policy for the device/port comes from: assigned → device default → org default → hardcoded fallback.
security.non_override_chain: Non-override ports fall through this chain:

actions_tab.tooltip_backup_tab: Manual backup (also in Config Backup tab).
actions_tab.tooltip_move_location: Move-location action is done via the 'Move Location' modal in the device list.
actions_tab.move_info: Use the "Move Location" action in the Devices list
actions_tab.archive_confirm_suffix: Archive this device? (Hidden from the list, can be restored.)

terminal.approval_message:
[APPROVAL REQUIRED] Request #{{id}} created. Awaiting admin approval.
Risk: {{risk}}
terminal.no_access_desc: Running commands or opening a live SSH session on this device requires the 'devices.connect' permission. Retry with org_admin+ role.
terminal.mode_repl_desc: Auditable command flow (approval + readonly + risk evaluation)
terminal.mode_ssh_desc: Full interactive SSH (vim/htop). Approval/audit are NOT in this flow.
terminal.readonly_tooltip_on: Read-only: show/ping only. Click → switch to write mode.
terminal.readonly_tooltip_off: Write mode: config commands active. Click → back to read-only.
terminal.approval_tooltip_on: Approval flow: config commands go to admin approval. Click → disable.
terminal.approval_tooltip_off: Approval flow disabled. Click → send config commands for admin approval.
terminal.empty_hint: Type a command and press Enter…

bulk_policy.title: Bulk assign port policy · {{count}} ports
bulk_policy.alert_msg: Selected port policy is assigned as override to {{count}} ports.
bulk_policy.alert_desc: If an override exists, the policy is changed (upsert). The action is atomic — if any port errors, none are written.

bulk_poe_restart.title: Bulk PoE Restart · {{count}} ports
bulk_poe_restart.flow_title: PoE Restart flow: disable → wait → enable
bulk_poe_restart.flow_desc: For each port the configured wait runs between two SSH commands. PoE-incompatible ports are skipped (counter). 10–15s recommended for APs / IP phones / cameras.
bulk_poe_restart.wait_extra: Time between disable → wait → enable. Leave empty to use server default.
bulk_poe_restart.rollback_extra: After this duration PoE stays on (rollback command enables). 0 → disables rollback timer (permanent).

bulk_vlan.title: Bulk assign VLAN · {{count}} ports
bulk_vlan.alert_msg: VLAN assignment is applied to {{count}} selected ports.
bulk_vlan.alert_desc: NOT atomic: separate SSH command per port; if one errors others still write. Error report is shown as message.

topology.title: Network Topology
topology.ghost_info: Saved credentials for the same vendor will be tried.
topology.ghost_info_desc: If successful, the device is added to inventory and LLDP neighbors are discovered. Otherwise, you will be asked to enter credentials.
topology.auto_login_desc: Enter username and password to connect to {{ip}}.
topology.cred_tried: {{count}} credentials tried, login failed. Please enter your credentials.
topology.hop_suggestion: You can expand the map by sending LLDP to these switches.
topology.blast_critical: CRITICAL — {{count}} devices will lose connectivity if this device goes offline
topology.blast_safe: Network is resilient — sub-networks will remain connected if this device goes offline

monitor.title: Alerts & Events
monitor.bulk_ack_confirm: {{count}} events will be acknowledged, are you sure?

tasks.title: Tasks
tasks.cancel_confirm: Are you sure you want to cancel this task?

agents.title: Proxy Agents
agents.create_desc: Once the agent is created, you can download a personalized installer script. Run it once — it registers as a service and starts automatically after reboot.
agents.delete_desc: Devices assigned to this agent may be affected.
agents.created_warning: The agent key is shown only once. Save it now!
agents.linux_hint: Paste the command into a terminal, or download the .sh file and run with sudo bash. Root access required.
agents.windows_hint: Paste the command into an elevated PowerShell window, or download the .ps1 file and run as administrator.
agents.server_url_label: Server Address (URL the agent will connect to)
agents.server_url_hint: If the agent runs on a different machine, enter the real IP or hostname. e.g. http://192.168.1.100:8000
agents.no_agents_desc: The agent is installed on your local network and handles all SSH communication. Install it once on an administrator's PC or server — it starts automatically on every reboot.

ip_allowlist.label: Allowed IPs (optional)
ip_allowlist.tooltip: User can log in only from these IP/CIDRs. Empty = no restriction. Separate multiple values with commas. Example: <ex>{{example}}</ex>

locations.intro: This list determines <s>which locations the user can see</s>. The authority level (admin/viewer) is set by the <s>role</s> field on the <e>General</e> tab.
locations.bullet_super_admin: • <s>Super Admin</s> accesses all locations of all organizations (no assignment needed).
locations.bullet_org_admin: • <s>Org Admin</s> automatically accesses all locations of their organization.
locations.bullet_location_admin: • <s>Location Admin</s> and <s>Viewer</s> only access the locations assigned below.
locations.fine_grained_hint: For finer-grained control (module × action) you can assign the user a custom permission set on the <permLink>Permission Settings</permLink> page.

invite.toast_token_not_stored: Token is not stored for security — create a new invite

modal.title: Create Invite Link
modal.success_share_hint: Share this link with the person you want to invite. The link is single-use.

diff.no_state: No state change for this record.

category.create: {{actor}} created a new record via {{action}} on {{target}}.
category.approve: {{actor}} performed an approval action via {{action}} on {{target}}.

resource.no_route: No detail page for this resource type
resource.no_id_for_detail: Resource ID missing for detail view

page.title: Audit Log
page.subtitle: Complete audit trail of user actions — who, when, what resource, from which IP.

empty.no_data_desc: When actions occur in the system, they will appear here.
empty.no_match_desc: No audit records match the current filters.

terminal_sessions.page_subtitle: Interactive SSH terminal sessions opened from the browser · extracted commands, IO bytes, output excerpt · forensic audit.

alert.ai_coming_title: AI summary feature coming soon (T9 Tur 3B)
alert.ai_coming_desc: Each session's command list + IO bytes + last ~10KB output is currently being recorded. AI session summarization (Claude API) will be enabled in the next increment.

ai.empty_title: No AI summary generated for this session yet.
ai.empty_provider_hint: AI Assistant uses the active provider (Settings → AI Assistant). You will get an error if no provider is configured.

commands.empty: No commands extracted (empty input or ongoing session)

toast.ai_failed: AI summary could not be generated

settings.title: Settings
settings.page_subtitle: General settings · notification channels · alert rules · SLA policies · credential & token management.

alert_rules.intro_message: Sends notifications when SNMP poll results exceed interface utilization or error thresholds.
alert_rules.intro_desc: Your notification channels must have the 'Threshold Alert (SNMP)' option enabled.

form.device_tooltip: If empty, applies to all devices
form.interface_pattern_tooltip: fnmatch pattern. E.g.: Gi0/*, Te*, empty = all interfaces
form.consecutive_tooltip: How many consecutive polls must exceed the threshold
form.cooldown_tooltip: Minimum notification interval for the same rule

notifications.intro: Manage notification channels. When events occur, messages are sent automatically to the selected channels.

teams.webhook_extra: Get it from the Incoming Webhook connector in the Teams channel

jira.info_alert: For Jira Cloud use an Atlassian API Token. For Jira Server enter username + password.
jira.api_token_extra: Atlassian Cloud: id.atlassian.com → Security → API token
jira.project_key_extra: Short code from the Jira project page (NET, OPS, INFRA, etc.)
jira.issue_type_extra: Issue type name defined in your Jira project

credentials.intro_message: Credentials are stored with Fernet encryption. Passwords are never returned in API responses.
credentials.intro_desc: Centralize credential management by assigning profiles to devices. Devices with assigned profiles use this profile for SSH/SNMP connections.

popconfirm.delete_desc: Cannot be deleted if devices are assigned to this profile.

form.name_placeholder: E.g.: Cisco Core, Aruba Access, Read-Only
form.enable_secret_tooltip: If empty, the current value is preserved; enter a space to clear

sla.intro: Define uptime targets per device or group. Notifications can be sent on breach.

popconfirm.delete_title: Do you want to delete this policy?

form.scope_devices_extra: If left empty, all devices are included

snmp.alert_ruijie_desc: Community string must include at least 3 character types: uppercase + lowercase + digit. Example: "Charon1"
snmp.alert_ssh_title: SNMP commands are sent directly to the device firmware over SSH
snmp.alert_ssh_desc: SSH connects to each device, enables SNMP, and writes the community string. Can take ~2-3 minutes for 58 devices.

form.community_extra: Ruijie requires uppercase + lowercase + digit — e.g. "Charon1"

snmp.btn_apply_to_all: Apply to All Devices via SSH
snmp.confirm_apply_all: Will SSH-connect to all {{count}} devices. Are you sure?
snmp.manual_poll_hint: Celery beat auto-polls every 5 minutes. This button queues immediately.
snmp.result_failed_hint: For failed devices use Device Detail → Health → Update SNMP.

toast.poll_queued: SNMP poll task queued — data will update in ~30 seconds

rotation.intro_message: SSH password rotation connects to real devices and changes the password.
rotation.intro_desc: Profile is not updated unless all devices succeed. Cisco IOS/IOS-XE and Ruijie are supported.
rotation.empty: No rotation policies yet. Add one from the profile buttons above.

maintenance.alert_active: {{count}} active maintenance window(s) — SNMP alerts are suppressed for these devices.
maintenance.alert_idle: SNMP threshold alerts are auto-suppressed during scheduled maintenance.
maintenance.alert_desc: While a maintenance window is active, AlertRule violations for the related devices do not send notifications.

recurrence.info_message: If you define a recurrence, sample windows are created automatically for the next 14 days.
recurrence.info_desc: The date/time range becomes a 'template'; sample windows use the same duration on each recurrence.
recurrence.label: Recurrence

form.devices_tooltip: Select devices in the maintenance scope

api_tokens.section_subtitle: Create long-lived tokens for automation and API access.

modal.warn_title: This token will never be shown again!
modal.warn_desc: Please copy it now and save it somewhere safe.

ai.security_message: Your API keys are stored on the server with Fernet encryption and never logged.

system.no_permission_desc: Only org_admin or super_admin can view and change system settings.
system.info_desc: Very low scan frequency overloads devices (especially SNMP); very high values slow down latency perception. Each setting has a recommended range below it; values outside min/max are not accepted.
system.scope_legend_global: applies to the entire platform (only super_admin can change). Some settings take effect after a Celery worker restart.

row.global_lock_tooltip: Global setting — only super_admin can change

popconfirm.reset_desc: This setting will return to its default value. Continue?

toast.worker_restart_required: ⚠ Some settings require Celery worker restart

retention.intro: If cleanup were running now, how many rows would be deleted from each table? This operation deletes nothing — it only counts. Actual cleanup runs in the daily beat task with per-org effective retention (plan cap + 7-day floor).
retention.nothing_to_delete: No old data to delete — everything is within the retention window.

password_policy.intro: These rules apply on new user creation, user password change, and after admin reset. Source:
password_policy.no_permission_desc: Only org_admin or super_admin can view and change the password policy.

popconfirm.reset_desc: This org will return to the global default. Continue?

form.history_tooltip: Prevent reuse of the last N passwords. 0 = no check.
form.expiry_tooltip: At the end of this period the user is forced to change their password. 0 = no expiry.
form.force_change_label: Force password change on first login

password_policy.tester_hint: Type below to see if a password passes the current policy.
password_policy.tester_fail_message: Password did not pass the policy

toast.reset_done: Org override policy deleted — global default applies

mfa.enabled_desc: Each login requires a 6-digit code from your authenticator app.
mfa.disable_warning: Your account will be single-factor again.
mfa.regen_warning: When new codes are generated, the old ones become invalid immediately.
mfa.setup_step2_title: 2 / 2 — Enter the code from your app
mfa.setup_intro_suffix: ) scan the QR or manually enter the setup key. This key is shown only once.
mfa.setup_qr_fallback_hint: If you cannot scan the QR, use "Enter setup key" → paste this text.
mfa.idle_desc: Your account is protected by password only. Enable TOTP with one click — works with Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator, Authy and 1Password.

recovery.panel_intro_pre: If you lose authenticator access, enter one of the codes below in the
recovery.panel_intro_mid: field on the Login page to sign back in. Each code is
recovery.panel_intro_post: . This list is shown only now.
recovery.file_one_time_notice: Each code is SINGLE-USE. Use one if you lose authenticator access.

email.active_desc: During login, OTP can be sent to your email instead of the authenticator. Authenticator can still be used as the primary method.
email.idle_desc: A 6-digit OTP is sent to your registered email address. Acts as an additional verification channel alongside the authenticator.
email.requirement_alert: To send email, your organization must have at least one active email notification channel defined (Settings → Notifications).
email.sent_desc: Enter the 6-digit code sent to your email. The code is valid for 10 minutes.

toast.enabled: MFA enabled — save your recovery codes now.
toast.regen_ok: New recovery codes generated — the old ones are now invalid.

help.title: Help & Getting Started
help.subtitle: Everything about using Charon and its features
help.qs_step1_desc: Go to Devices page and click + Add Device. Enter the IP address, SSH credentials and vendor type.
help.qs_step2_desc: For access from different network segments, go to Agents page and install a proxy agent.
help.qs_step3_desc: In the Topology page, click 'LLDP Discover'. Neighboring switches are discovered automatically.
help.qs_step4_desc: Create automatic alert rules from Alert Rules page and track events from Monitor.
help.feat_dashboard_desc: Overall network health score, online/offline device counts, recent events and quick actions.
help.feat_devices_desc: SSH connectivity, port status, bulk credential updates and configuration management.
help.feat_topology_desc: Automatic network map via LLDP, layer filtering, ghost switch discovery and blast radius analysis.
help.feat_twin_desc: Takes a snapshot (baseline) of your network topology and automatically compares every change. 'New Connection' means an unauthorized cable or device; 'Lost Connection' means a disconnected link. If changes were planned, click 'New Baseline' to accept the current state. Access via Topology → Digital Twin.
help.feat_monitor_desc: Real-time alerts for device offline, CPU/memory threshold breaches and other critical events.
help.feat_backups_desc: Automatic or manual device config backups with version diff analysis.
help.feat_ipam_desc: IP address management, subnet planning and utilization monitoring.
help.feat_vlan_desc: Create, delete and apply VLANs to switches.
help.feat_compliance_desc: Automatically verify that configurations meet security and standards compliance rules.
help.feat_bandwidth_desc: Per-port traffic monitoring via SNMP with ingress/egress graphs and utilization analytics.
help.feat_security_desc: Detect security vulnerabilities, weak passwords and misconfigurations in your network.
help.feat_playbooks_desc: Automate repetitive network tasks. Supports scheduled or event-based triggering.
help.feat_change_desc: Plan, approve and execute network changes with a full audit trail.
help.feat_sla_desc: Device and service uptime tracking, SLA compliance reports and trend analysis.
help.feat_agents_desc: Distributed agent infrastructure for SSH access across different network segments.
help.feat_racks_desc: Visually manage physical server rack and hardware placements.
help.feat_floor_desc: Position and visualize devices on building floor plans.
help.feat_diagnostics_desc: Real-time network diagnostics with ping, traceroute and port scanning tools.
help.feat_reports_desc: Inventory, event history and backup status reports with CSV export.
help.feat_snmptrap_desc: Real-time linkDown/linkUp event capture from devices via agent UDP 1620.
help.feat_maintenance_desc: Suppress alerts during scheduled downtime; one-shot or recurring (daily/weekly/monthly).
help.feat_terminal_desc: Browser-based SSH terminal via agent relay; dangerous commands gated, all keystrokes audited.
help.feat_syslog_desc: Agent collects syslog on UDP 514; facility/severity filtering, per-device view.
help.feat_poe_desc: Side menu → PoE / Energy. Per-device live PoE power draw (Watts). Background SNMP snapshot every 15 minutes via POWER-ETHERNET-MIB; for real mW open the device drawer and click 'Live SSH (Real W)' — runs 'show poe interfaces status' (Ruijie) or 'show power inline' (Cisco). Each port shows PD class (1-8), state, power, last update. 'Fetch (Live)' button parallel-SNMP probes the entire fleet.
help.feat_firmware_desc: Side menu → Firmware (org_admin+). Two tabs: Catalog + Install Jobs. Add firmware via file upload (SHA256 streamed) or URL catalog (TFTP/HTTP/SCP). Install worker takes a PRE-RESTORE snapshot, transfers + boot-set + save, then pauses at 'awaiting_reload'. Operator approves the reboot from the drawer; post-version is verified after the device comes back.
help.feat_configbuilder_desc: Side menu → Easy Config Builder. Add VLAN / tag a port / set NTP-syslog-SNMP-DNS without writing CLI. Vendor-aware command generation with live CLI box. Preview with vendor/OS only — no device needed; download as .txt. Batch: add multiple operations to the cart, apply all at once. 9 operations × 5 vendor families (Cisco/Aruba/Ruijie/HP/Comware).
help.feat_configrollback_desc: Backup Center → expand a device → click RollbackOutlined next to a backup. Side-by-side 'active vs target' cards + live diff preview. On confirm, a PRE-RESTORE snapshot is taken first (safety), then the old config is pushed and write memory runs. Skipped if device is already on the target hash.
help.feat_bulkops_desc: Devices page: select rows, then toolbar offers Tag · Lifecycle (production/passive/stock/archived) · Move Location · Assign Agent · Update Password · Bulk Backup · Fetch Info · Delete. Each bulk action is audit-logged; failed devices appear in a 'skipped' list with reasons.
help.feat_sshaudit_desc: Side menu → SSH Session Audit. Records every interactive SSH session opened from the browser — user, device, start/end, extracted commands (backspace/ESC sequences cleaned), IO byte counts, last ~10KB output excerpt, exit reason. Idle sessions over 30 min auto-close as 'stale_cleanup'.
help.feat_aisummary_desc: SSH Session Audit drawer → 'Generate AI Summary' button. Sends the command list + output excerpt to your AI provider (Claude / OpenAI / Gemini / Ollama — picked in Settings) and returns a 2-3 sentence summary of what the operator did. Forensic audit + reporting.
help.feat_porttoggle_desc: Devices → ApiOutlined icon on a device card → /devices/{id}/ports. Per-port On/Off + PoE On/Off buttons. Click opens a 'reason' textarea modal with 5-min auto-rollback timer. Confirm within the timer = permanent; otherwise the system auto-runs the inverse command (safety pattern: prevents accidental self-lockout).
help.feat_cyclicmw_desc: Settings → Maintenance Windows → New Window → 'Recurrence' section: Daily/Weekly/Monthly. For weekly pick the days, for monthly pick day-of-month (1-28). An hourly beat task materializes the next 14 days of instances; each child instance behaves as a normal one-shot window for alert suppression.
help.feat_systemset_desc: Settings → System Settings tab (super_admin). Scan sensitivity (timeout/retry/threshold), maintenance-during-scan behavior, agent revalidate interval, snapshot retention, etc. All settings are JSON-typed with cache + audit trail.
help.feat_pwdpolicy_desc: Settings → Password Policy tab (org_admin). Min length, complexity (upper/lower/digit/symbol), history reuse ban (last N), forced rotation in days. Login response carries password_expired flag and forces the change screen.
help.feat_mfaemail_desc: Profile → Multi-Factor Authentication tab. After enrolling TOTP (Google Authenticator etc.) you can also enable Email OTP. Login offers method picker; 6-digit OTP valid 10 minutes, bcrypt-hashed in DB, single-use.
help.feat_ipallowlist_desc: Profile → IP Allowlist tab. Restrict your account to specific IPs / CIDRs. CSV: '10.0.0.0/8, 192.168.1.5'. 'Add My Current IP' button for quick fill. Self-lockout protection: if your current request IP isn't in the new list, save is rejected with 409. Empty = no restriction.
help.feat_ipamtools_desc: IPAM → Subnet drawer: 'Fill from ARP' (pulls device ARP caches, adds matching IPs as assignments, auto-fills hostnames from your Device table) and 'IP Scan (Ping)' (parallel ICMP sweep, adds responding IPs as 'discovery' source, removes the ones that no longer respond — manual entries untouched). Quick way to populate a fresh subnet.
help.role_super_admin_desc: Full access to all permissions and all organizations.
help.role_admin_desc: Full operations within own organization including user management.
help.role_operator_desc: Device, task and configuration management. Cannot create users.
help.role_viewer_desc: Read-only access. Cannot make any changes.
